subtopics.title: What's in the Box
subtopics.content:
#### Player-Count Audit
- **Supported counts** — 1 to 4 players.
- **Scaling** — The companion app adjusts enemy count and difficulty based on player count.
- **Solo mode** — Fully supported; control 1-2 heroes.

#### Required App
- **Descent companion app** — Required for play; manages enemies, story, map reveals, and combat resolution.
- **Platforms** — Available on iOS, Android, and Steam.

#### Heroes (6 Available)
- **Hero figures** — 6 detailed plastic hero miniatures.
- **Hero cards** — Each hero has a unique set of skill cards.
- **Weapon cards** — Double-sided weapon cards that flip between two configurations.

#### 3D Terrain
- **Terrain pieces** — 3D cardboard terrain: walls, doors, staircases, bookshelves, barrels, trees, and more.
- **Map tiles** — Modular map tiles assembled as directed by the app.
- **Elevation** — 3D terrain creates multi-level environments.

#### Enemy Components
- **Enemy figures** — Plastic miniatures for various enemy types.
- **Enemy activation** — Managed entirely by the app.

#### Other Components
- **Condition tokens** — Various status effect tokens (poison, stun, etc.).
- **Fatigue tokens** — Track hero fatigue.
- **Damage tokens** — Track damage on enemies and heroes.
- **Exploration tokens** — Mark points of interest on the map.
- **Loot tokens** — Collectible treasure during quests.

subtopics.title: Board Layout
subtopics.content:
#### Quest Setup
1. **Launch app** — Open the companion app and select the quest.
2. **App instructions** — The app guides all setup step by step.
3. **Place map tiles** — Assemble map tiles as shown by the app (the map reveals progressively during play).
4. **Build 3D terrain** — Place 3D terrain pieces on the tiles as directed.
5. **Place tokens** — Add exploration tokens, doors, and other markers per app instructions.
6. **Place heroes** — Set hero miniatures on starting positions shown in the app.
7. **Initial enemies** — Place enemy figures where the app indicates.

#### Progressive Reveal
- **Fog of war** — The full map is not shown at start; new areas are revealed as heroes explore.
- **App manages layout** — The app tells you exactly which tiles and terrain to place when areas are revealed.

subtopics.title: Player Setup
subtopics.content:
#### Hero Selection
- **Choose heroes** — Each player selects a hero from the 6 available.
- **Skill cards** — Take your hero's starting skill cards.
- **Weapon cards** — Equip starting weapons (each weapon card has 2 sides with different abilities).
- **Fatigue track** — Set fatigue to 0.
- **Health** — Set health to your hero's maximum as shown in the app.

#### Campaign Continuity
- **Persistent heroes** — Heroes carry skills, weapons, and upgrades between quests.
- **Crafted items** — Equipment crafted between quests is available at the start of each quest.
- **App tracks progress** — All campaign state is saved in the app.

--- 1 Player ---
- **Solo play** — Control 1 or 2 heroes; the app adjusts difficulty.

--- 2-4 Players ---
- **One hero each** — Each player controls exactly 1 hero.

subtopics.title: Starting the Game
subtopics.content:
#### Campaign Start
- **Tutorial quest** — The first quest serves as a tutorial, introducing core mechanics.
- **Narrative intro** — The app presents the story narrative before and during quests.
- **16 quests** — The main campaign consists of 16 quests with branching story paths.

#### Each Quest Start
- **App narration** — Read or listen to the quest introduction in the app.
- **Setup map** — Follow app instructions to build the starting area.
- **Select skills** — Choose which skill cards to bring for this quest.
- **Begin play** — The app directs when to start and manages turn flow.

subtopics.title: Turn Structure
subtopics.content:
#### Round Structure
1. **Hero phase** — Each hero takes a turn in any player-chosen order.
2. **Darkness phase** — The app activates enemies, advances the threat timer, and triggers story events.

#### Hero Turn
- **3 actions** — Each hero gets 3 actions per turn.
- **Action types** — Move, attack, interact, use skill, ready weapon, explore.
- **Repeat actions** — You may take the same action multiple times.
- **Free actions** — Some abilities are free actions and do not cost an action.

#### Darkness Phase
- **App-controlled** — Press the end-turn button in the app.
- **Enemy activation** — The app shows each enemy's actions (movement and attacks).
- **Story events** — Narrative events and environmental changes may trigger.
- **Threat advancement** — The threat/doom tracker may advance, adding urgency.

subtopics.title: Actions & Options
subtopics.content:
#### Movement
- **Move action** — Move up to your speed value in spaces.
- **Terrain interaction** — 3D terrain affects movement (stairs, blocked paths, elevated positions).
- **Elevation** — Moving between levels costs additional movement.

#### Combat
- **Attack action** — Use your equipped weapon to attack an enemy.
- **Weapon sides** — Each weapon card has 2 sides; flip to access different attack abilities.
- **Dice pool** — Roll dice based on your weapon and skill cards.
- **App resolution** — Input results into the app; it calculates damage and effects.
- **Surge results** — Special surge symbols on dice trigger bonus effects.

#### Interaction
- **Explore tokens** — Interact with exploration tokens to discover items, secrets, or story elements.
- **Doors** — Open doors to reveal new areas of the map.
- **Objects** — Interact with 3D terrain objects for various effects.

#### Skills
- **Skill cards** — Play skill cards for special abilities during your turn.
- **Exhaust** — Used skill cards are exhausted (flipped face down) until recovered.
- **Fatigue cost** — Some powerful skills cost fatigue to activate.

#### Ready Weapon
- **Flip weapon** — Spend an action to flip your weapon card to its other side, changing your available attack.

subtopics.title: Special Mechanics
subtopics.content:
#### Fatigue System
- **Pushing limits** — Heroes can gain fatigue to power abilities or push beyond normal limits.
- **Fatigue track** — As fatigue accumulates, heroes become weaker.
- **Fatigue threshold** — Crossing fatigue thresholds applies penalties.
- **Recovery** — Fatigue can be reduced through rest actions and certain skills.

#### Weapon Flipping
- **Dual configuration** — Every weapon has 2 sides representing different fighting styles or modes.
- **Tactical choice** — Flipping between sides mid-quest adapts to different combat situations.
- **Upgrade paths** — Weapons can be upgraded between quests, improving both sides.

#### App-Driven Narrative
- **Branching story** — Decisions made during quests affect the campaign narrative.
- **Hidden information** — The app manages enemy AI, hidden rooms, and surprise events.
- **Puzzle elements** — Some quests include puzzles managed by the app.
- **Save system** — Campaign progress saves automatically in the app.

#### Crafting System
- **Between quests** — Use materials found during quests to craft and upgrade equipment.
- **Recipes** — Discover crafting recipes throughout the campaign.
- **Upgrade weapons** — Improve weapon cards to unlock stronger abilities on both sides.

subtopics.title: Endgame & Scoring
subtopics.content:
#### Quest Victory
- **Complete objective** — Each quest has objectives managed by the app (defeat a boss, escape, investigate, etc.).
- **App declares outcome** — The app announces victory or defeat based on game state.

#### Quest Defeat
- **All heroes defeated** — If all heroes are knocked out, the quest is lost.
- **Timer expired** — Some quests have a doom/threat timer; running out means failure.
- **Retry** — Failed quests can be replayed; the campaign continues either way with narrative consequences.

#### Campaign Completion
- **16 quests** — The main campaign concludes after the final quest.
- **Branching endings** — Campaign choices affect the ending narrative.
- **No competitive scoring** — Fully cooperative with no VP or scoring system.
- **Replayability** — Different choices lead to different quest paths and outcomes.

subtopics.title: Opening Priorities
subtopics.content:
- **Explore early** — Revealing the map quickly gives your team more tactical options.
- **Manage fatigue** — Do not exhaust your heroes in the first few rooms; pace your fatigue spending.
- **Coordinate hero order** — Choose turn order each round to maximize synergy (tanks go first, support follows).
- **Flip weapons situationally** — Switch weapon sides based on whether you need area damage, single-target power, or utility.
- **Interact with everything** — Exploration tokens often provide critical items or story progression.

subtopics.title: Common Mistakes
subtopics.content:
- **Ignoring fatigue management** — Overusing fatigue abilities early leaves heroes weakened for boss encounters.
- **Not flipping weapons** — Sticking to one weapon side misses half your tactical options.
- **Rushing through rooms** — Leaving exploration tokens uncollected means missing items and story content.
- **Poor turn order** — Not thinking about hero activation order wastes potential combos.
- **Forgetting 3D terrain rules** — Elevation and terrain features affect movement and line of sight.

subtopics.title: Key Decisions
subtopics.content:
#### Decision 1: Speed vs Thoroughness
- **Rush objectives** — Minimize time in dangerous areas to reduce damage taken.
- **Explore fully** — Clear rooms and interact with everything for maximum rewards.

#### Decision 2: Fatigue Spending
- **Push now** — Spend fatigue for powerful abilities in current combat.
- **Conserve** — Save fatigue capacity for harder encounters later in the quest.

#### Decision 3: Crafting Priorities
- **Upgrade weapons** — Improve combat effectiveness for the whole campaign.
- **Craft items** — Consumable items provide flexible solutions to varied challenges.
